result.summary: GitLab.com exposes a REST API, a GraphQL API, the `glab` CLI, and an experimental local MCP server via `glab mcp serve`, authenticated primarily with OAuth apps, personal/project/group access tokens, and in some cases CI job tokens.
result.description: GitLab is a web-based platform for source code hosting, collaboration, CI/CD, and project management. GitLab.com is the hosted SaaS offering of GitLab.

gitlab_oauth_app.label: GitLab OAuth application (client ID + client secret)
gitlab_oauth_app.setup: Create an OAuth app in GitLab: go to [Applications](https://gitlab.com/-/profile/applications), select **Add new application**, enter a name and `Redirect URI`, choose the needed OAuth scopes, then save. GitLab shows an **Application ID** (client ID) and **Secret**. GitLab also supports dynamic client registration at [oauth/register](https://gitlab.com/oauth/register). OAuth authorize/token endpoints are `https://gitlab.com/oauth/authorize` and `https://gitlab.com/oauth/token`. For GitLab.com, `glab` can also use OAuth login via `glab auth login --web` or device flow via `glab auth login --device`.

gitlab_pat.label: GitLab personal access token
gitlab_pat.setup: In GitLab.com, open [Personal access tokens](https://gitlab.com/-/user_settings/personal_access_tokens), choose **Generate token** > **Legacy token**, set a name, expiration, and scopes such as `read_api` or `api`, then create the token and copy it before leaving the page.

gitlab_project_token.label: GitLab project access token
gitlab_project_token.setup: Open the target project in GitLab.com, then go to **Settings** > **Access tokens** as described in [Project access tokens](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/project/settings/project_access_tokens/). Create a token with the required role and scopes, then copy it when shown. On GitLab.com this requires Premium or Ultimate.

gitlab_group_token.label: GitLab group access token
gitlab_group_token.setup: Open the target group in GitLab.com, then go to **Settings** > **Access tokens** as described in [Group access tokens](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/group/settings/group_access_tokens/). Create a token with the required role and scopes, then copy it when shown. On GitLab.com this requires Premium or Ultimate and is not available during trial.

gitlab_ci_job_token.label: GitLab CI/CD job token
gitlab_ci_job_token.setup: Run the integration from a GitLab CI/CD job. GitLab exposes the token in the job environment as `CI_JOB_TOKEN`; docs note it only works for specific API endpoints. For `glab`, you can log in with `glab auth login --job-token $CI_JOB_TOKEN` or rely on CI auto-login where supported.
